# Rules that keep this file valid YAML when values are generated by automation:
# 1. Wrap every string value in double quotes, even if it looks safe without them.
# 2. Apostrophes (a trader's edge) need no escaping inside double quotes.
# 3. A literal double quote inside a value must be escaped as \".
# 4. Colons inside a quoted value (e.g. "Oil: A Primer") are safe.
# 5. Do not put literal line breaks inside a frontmatter value. Keep every

#    Markdown body below the closing "---", not in frontmatter.

#    valid YAML. This is the easiest style to generate directly from a
#    JSON value in an automation tool.

title: "Example Headline That Matches The Article's Main Point"
description: "One or two sentences summarizing the article. This is used as the meta description and the deck text shown under the headline."

---

Open with a direct answer to the question implied by the title or headline. Do not
build up to it. State the point in the first one or two sentences, then use the
rest of the article to explain it.

## A clear subheading

Body paragraphs go here. Use plain Markdown: paragraphs, `##`/`###` subheadings,
`-` bullet lists, and `>` blockquotes as needed. Do not use literal em dashes or
long dash punctuation anywhere in the visible text.

## Another subheading

- Keep list items short and direct.
- Define any term the first time it is used.

Close with a short concluding paragraph rather than trailing off.
